NCT01691521.name: MENSA
NCT01691521.group: Severe eosinophilic asthma, mepolizumab 100 mg SC vs placebo Q4W

allOutcomes.title: Annualized rate of clinically significant exacerbations (rate ratio)

NCT01691521.snippet: Source: Ortega HG et al. NEJM 2014;371:1198-1207 (MENSA).

evidence.label: Primary — Exacerbation Rate
evidence.text: Mepolizumab 100 mg SC reduced annualized rate of clinically significant exacerbations from 1.74 to 0.83 (rate ratio 0.47; 95% CI 0.35-0.64; P<0.001). 53% reduction. n=385 mepolizumab vs 191 placebo (analyzed 576 total 32-week trial).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 12 Years; healthy volunteers: f. NCT01914757.name: CALIMA
NCT01914757.group: Severe eosinophilic asthma, benralizumab 30 mg SC Q8W vs placebo

allOutcomes.title: Annualized exacerbation rate (rate ratio Q8W vs placebo)

NCT01914757.snippet: Source: FitzGerald JM et al. Lancet 2016;388:2128-2141 (CALIMA).

evidence.label: Primary — Exacerbation Rate
evidence.text: Benralizumab 30 mg Q8W reduced annualized exacerbation rate 36% vs placebo (rate ratio 0.64; 95% CI 0.49-0.85; P=0.0018) in eosinophilic stratum. 56-week trial, 1,306 patients total randomized.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 12 Years; max age 75 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~1. Provision of informed consent prior to any study specific procedures~2. Female and male aged 12 to 75 years, inclusively, at the time of Visit 1~3. History of physician-diagnosed asthma requiring treatment with medium-to-high dose ICS (>250µg fluticasone dry powder formulation equivalents total daily dose) and a LABA, for at least 12 months prior to Visit 1.~4. NCT02414854.name: QUEST
NCT02414854.group: Uncontrolled moderate-severe asthma, dupilumab 200 mg SC Q2W vs placebo

allOutcomes.title: Annualized severe exacerbation rate (rate ratio, all-comer population)

NCT02414854.snippet: Source: Castro M et al. NEJM 2018;378:2486-2496 (QUEST).

evidence.label: Primary — Exacerbation Rate
evidence.text: Dupilumab 200 mg Q2W reduced annualized severe exacerbation rate 47.7% vs placebo (rate ratio 0.52; 95% CI 0.41-0.66; P<0.001) in all-comer population. Greater benefit in type-2-high subgroup (eosinophil >=300, fraction of exhaled NO).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 12 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion criteria:~-Adults and adolescent participants with a physician diagnosis of asthma for ≥12 months, based on the Global Initiative for Asthma (GINA) 2014 Guidelines and the following criteria:~a) Existing treatment with medium to high dose ICS (≥250 mcg of fluticasone propionate twice daily or equipotent ICS daily dosage to a maximum of 2000 mcg/day of fluticasone propionate or equivalent) in combination with a second controller (eg, long-acting beta agonist, leukotriene receptor antag...

NCT03347279.name: NAVIGATOR
NCT03347279.group: Severe uncontrolled asthma, tezepelumab 210 mg SC Q4W vs placebo
NCT03347279.snippet: Source: Menzies-Gow A et al. NEJM 2021;384:1800-1809 (NAVIGATOR).

evidence.label: Primary — Exacerbation Rate
evidence.text: Tezepelumab 210 mg Q4W reduced annualized rate of asthma exacerbations from 2.10 to 0.93 (rate ratio 0.44; 95% CI 0.37-0.53; P<0.001). 56% reduction. Benefit preserved in low-eosinophil subgroup (rate ratio 0.59), first biologic effective across biomarker phenotypes.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 12 Years; max age 80 Years; healthy volunteers: f. title: RapidMeta Respiratory | Biologics in Severe Asthma (rate ratio) v1.0
